title: Fermented Kenyan Cheddar Bake
description: A springy, high-protein kenyan dish featuring mature cheddar, perfect for post-workout meals. This fermented bake combines traditional kenyan flavors with a modern twist, making it a great option for jain-friendly diets.
instructions: Combine mature cheddar cheese, whole wheat flour, chickpea flour, salt, and black pepper in a large mixing bowl.
Gradually add water to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Transfer the dough to a greased baking sheet, shaping it into a round or oblong shape.
Bake the dough in a preheated oven at 180°C for 20 minutes, or until it is golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the bake from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Mix fermented yogurt, chopped fresh cilantro, and chopped fresh scallions in a small bowl.
Spread the yogurt mixture over the top of the bake, making sure to cover the entire surface.
Return the bake to the oven for an additional 5 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
Remove the bake from the oven and let it cool completely before serving.
